Dubai Business Setup System

This project contains 7 agents, 12 tools, 4 knowledge sources.

Project Details

Description

Multi-agent system that guides entrepreneurs through the complete process of opening a business in Dubai, UAE — from jurisdiction selection and trade licensing through visa processing, bank account opening, and ongoing regulatory compliance.

Business Justification

Reduce business setup time from 8-12 weeks to 3-4 weeks by automating document preparation, government portal interactions, and compliance tracking. Eliminate costly consultant fees averaging AED 15,000-30,000.

  • Target Completion: Q3 2026
  • Estimated Effort: 12 weeks (3 engineers + 1 domain expert)
  • Team Size: 3 engineers + 1 UAE business setup consultant + 1 PM
  • Integration Points: DET (Dubai Economy & Tourism), GDRFA, MOHRE, FTA, Ejari/Dubai Land Department, UAE banking APIs (Emirates NBD, ADCB)
  • Deployment Target: Cloud
  • Overall Risk: Medium
  • Compliance: UAE Federal AML/CFT Law, Economic Substance Regulations, Corporate Tax Law, VAT Law, UBO regulations
  • Data Classification: Contains PII (passport scans, Emirates IDs), financial data (bank details, tax records), and legal documents (MOA/AOA)
  • Regulatory Constraints: Must comply with UAE Data Protection Law (Federal Decree-Law No. 45/2021). Document storage must be in UAE-based data centres.

Critical Dependencies

Government portal availability (DET, GDRFA, MOHRE), banking partner API access, real-time fee schedule updates

Key Assumptions

Client has valid passport with 6+ months validity. Business activity is permitted under UAE law. Client has sufficient capital for chosen jurisdiction.

Open Questions / Blockers

Should the system handle free zone to mainland conversion? Multi-emirate expansion support needed? Integration with PRO (Public Relations Officer) services?

Agents

Agent

Business Setup Orchestrator

Central coordinator that guides the client through the entire Dubai business setup process, from initial consultation to final compliance registration.

  • Framework: Custom
  • Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  • Role: Process orchestrator
  • Goal: Guide clients end-to-end through Dubai company formation, ensuring all steps are completed in the correct order with proper documentation
  • Memory: Both
  • Max Iterations: 20
  • Delegation: Enabled
Instructions
  1. Collect client requirements (business activity, budget, headcount). 2. Delegate to Jurisdiction Advisor for mainland vs free zone recommendation.
  2. Coordinate License Agent for trade name and license.
  3. Ensure Document Agent validates all paperwork.
  4. Hand off to Visa Agent for residence visas.
  5. Trigger Banking Agent for account opening.
  6. Engage Compliance Agent for ongoing obligations.

Report progress at each stage.

Agent

Jurisdiction Advisor

Analyzes the client's business activity, ownership needs, and budget to recommend the optimal jurisdiction: mainland (DET) or a specific free zone (DMCC, JAFZA, DAFZA, DIFC, etc.).

  • Framework: Custom
  • Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  • Role: Jurisdiction and legal form specialist
  • Goal: Recommend the best jurisdiction and legal structure (LLC, sole establishment, branch, free zone entity) based on client requirements
  • Memory: Both
  • Max Iterations: 10
  • Delegation: Enabled
Instructions

Evaluate business activity against DED activity list. Check if 100% foreign ownership is needed (mainland now allows this for most activities). Compare free zone benefits (DMCC for commodities, DIFC for financial services, DAFZA for tech/trade). Consider: visa allocation, office requirements, capital needs, and ability to trade on mainland. Return a structured recommendation with pros/cons.

Agent

Document Preparation Agent

Collects, validates, and organises all required documents for each stage of the business setup: passport copies, NOCs, MOA/AOA, Ejari certificates, and attestations.

  • Framework: Custom
  • Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  • Role: Document collection and validation specialist
  • Goal: Ensure all documents are complete, properly attested, and formatted before submission to government portals
  • Memory: Both
  • Max Iterations: 10
  • Delegation: Enabled
Instructions

For each stage, retrieve the required document checklist from the Document Checklist Library. Request documents from the client. Validate: passport validity (6+ months), attestation status, translation requirements (Arabic for mainland). Flag missing or expired documents immediately. Package documents for submission.

Agent

License & Registration Agent

Handles trade name reservation, initial approval, memorandum of association drafting, and trade license application through the DET or relevant free zone authority.

  • Framework: Custom
  • Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  • Role: Trade license and company registration specialist
  • Goal: Secure trade name approval and obtain the commercial/professional trade license
  • Memory: Both
  • Max Iterations: 10
  • Delegation: Enabled
Instructions
  1. Check trade name availability via Trade Name API.
  2. Submit initial approval application with business activity codes.
  3. If mainland: draft MOA/AOA for LLC, get notarised.
  4. Submit license application with all documents.
  5. Pay fees and track application status.
  6. Collect trade license.

For free zones: follow the specific free zone's registration portal workflow.

Agent

Visa & Immigration Agent

Manages the full immigration workflow: establishment card, employment visas, residence permits, Emirates ID registration, and medical fitness tests via GDRFA and MOHRE.

  • Framework: Custom
  • Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  • Role: Immigration and visa processing specialist
  • Goal: Obtain residence visas and Emirates IDs for company owners, partners, and employees
  • Memory: Both
  • Max Iterations: 10
  • Delegation: Enabled
Instructions
  1. Apply for establishment/immigration card via GDRFA.
  1. Submit work permit applications via MOHRE for each employee.
  1. Coordinate medical fitness tests at DHA-approved centres.
  1. Submit residence visa applications.
  1. Register for Emirates ID.
  1. Track status of each application.

Handle investor/partner visas (2-year or 10-year golden visa if eligible).

Agent

Banking & Finance Agent

Assists with corporate bank account opening, VAT registration with the Federal Tax Authority, and corporate tax enrolment. Coordinates KYC documentation.

  • Framework: Custom
  • Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  • Role: Banking and tax registration specialist
  • Goal: Open a corporate bank account and complete all tax registrations (VAT + Corporate Tax)
  • Memory: Both
  • Max Iterations: 10
  • Delegation: Enabled
Instructions
  1. Prepare bank account application package (trade license, MOA, passport copies, proof of address, business plan).
  2. Schedule appointments with recommended banks (Emirates NBD, ADCB, Mashreq, RAKBANK).
  3. Submit VAT registration if revenue exceeds AED 375,000 (mandatory) or AED 187,500 (voluntary).
  4. Register for Corporate Tax (9% on profits above AED 375,000). 5. Obtain Tax Registration Number (TRN).
Agent

Compliance Agent

Monitors and manages ongoing regulatory obligations: AML compliance, Economic Substance Regulations (ESR), Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO) declarations, annual license renewal, and audit requirements.

  • Framework: Custom
  • Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  • Role: Regulatory compliance specialist
  • Goal: Ensure the company remains compliant with all UAE federal and emirate-level regulations on an ongoing basis
  • Memory: Both
  • Max Iterations: 10
  • Delegation: Enabled
Instructions
  1. Set up AML/CFT compliance programme (appoint MLRO, KYC procedures, transaction monitoring).
  2. File annual ESR notification and report if applicable.
  3. Submit UBO declaration to registrar.
  4. Schedule trade license renewal 30 days before expiry.
  5. Coordinate annual audit if required.
  6. Monitor regulatory changes from MOE, CBUAE, and SCA.
  7. File corporate tax returns by the deadline.

Tools

Tool

DET Portal API

Dubai Economy & Tourism (formerly DED) portal for trade name reservation, initial approval, and trade license issuance on the mainland.

  • Type: API
  • Execution: Sync
  • Inputs: business_activity_code: String, trade_name: String, legal_form: String, partners: [Partner], documents: [Document]
  • Outputs: application_id: String, status: Enum(pending|approved|rejected), trade_license_number: String, fees: Decimal
  • Auth Method: OAuth
  • Endpoint: https://services.det.gov.ae/api/v1
  • Timeout: 60
  • Error Handling: Retry
Tool

GDRFA e-Services

General Directorate of Residency and Foreigners Affairs portal for visa applications, establishment cards, and residence permit processing.

  • Type: API
  • Execution: Sync
  • Inputs: application_type: Enum(establishment_card|entry_permit|residence_visa|visa_renewal), applicant: PersonDetails, sponsor: CompanyDetails, documents: [Document]
  • Outputs: application_id: String, status: Enum(submitted|under_review|approved|collected), permit_number: String, expiry_date: Date
  • Auth Method: OAuth
  • Endpoint: https://smart.gdrfad.gov.ae/api/v2
  • Timeout: 90
  • Error Handling: Retry
Tool

MOHRE Portal

Ministry of Human Resources and Emiratisation portal for work permits, labour cards, and employment contract registration.

  • Type: API
  • Execution: Sync
  • Inputs: permit_type: Enum(new_work_permit|renewal|cancellation), employee: EmployeeDetails, company: CompanyDetails, offer_letter: Document
  • Outputs: work_permit_number: String, status: Enum(pending|approved|rejected), labour_card_number: String, validity: DateRange
  • Auth Method: OAuth
  • Endpoint: https://mohre.gov.ae/api/v1
  • Timeout: 60
  • Error Handling: Retry
Tool

FTA Portal

Federal Tax Authority portal for VAT registration, corporate tax enrolment, TRN issuance, and tax return filing.

  • Type: API
  • Execution: Sync
  • Inputs: registration_type: Enum(vat|corporate_tax|excise), company: CompanyDetails, revenue_estimate: Decimal, financial_year_end: Date
  • Outputs: trn: String, registration_status: Enum(active|pending|suspended), next_filing_date: Date, return_id: String
  • Auth Method: OAuth
  • Endpoint: https://tax.gov.ae/api/v1
  • Timeout: 45
  • Error Handling: Retry
Tool

Ejari System

Dubai Land Department's tenancy contract registration system. Required for mainland companies to register their office lease.

  • Type: API
  • Execution: Sync
  • Inputs: contract_type: Enum(commercial|residential), tenant: CompanyDetails, landlord: LandlordDetails, property: PropertyDetails, lease_document: Document
  • Outputs: ejari_number: String, registration_date: Date, expiry_date: Date, certificate_url: URL
  • Auth Method: Bearer Token
  • Endpoint: https://ejari.dubai.ae/api/v1
  • Timeout: 30
  • Error Handling: Retry
Tool

Trade Name Check API

Real-time trade name availability search across DET and all UAE free zones. Returns naming conflicts and suggestions.

  • Type: API
  • Execution: Sync
  • Inputs: proposed_name: String, jurisdiction: Enum(mainland|dmcc|jafza|dafza|difc|dwtc|all), activity_code: String
  • Outputs: available: Boolean, conflicts: [String], suggestions: [String], reservation_fee: Decimal
  • Auth Method: Bearer Token
  • Endpoint: https://services.det.gov.ae/api/v1/tradename/check
  • Timeout: 10
  • Error Handling: Retry
Tool

Observability Service

Structured logging, distributed tracing, and alerting. Tracks latency, token usage, and error rates per agent step.

  • Type: API
  • Execution: Sync
  • Inputs: Not provided
  • Outputs: Not provided
  • Auth Method: Bearer Token
  • Endpoint: Not provided
  • Timeout: Not provided
  • Error Handling: Retry
Tool

  • Auth Method: Bearer Token

Knowledge Sources

Knowledge

UAE Business Regulations

Low

Comprehensive database of UAE federal laws, Dubai emirate decrees, and ministerial resolutions governing company formation, commercial licensing, and foreign investment.

  • Risk: Low
  • Data Formats: PDF, JSON, HTML
  • Size: ~2,500 legal documents, updated quarterly
  • Location: Azure Blob Storage (UAE North)
  • Access Method: RAG with vector embeddings (text-embedding-3-large)
  • Sensitivity: public
  • Update Frequency: Quarterly (aligned with gazette publications)
  • Versioning: Git-based with tagged releases per gazette issue
Knowledge

Free Zone Comparison Guide

Low

Detailed comparison matrix of 30+ UAE free zones covering: setup costs, visa allocations, office requirements, permitted activities, ownership rules, and regulatory benefits.

  • Risk: Low
  • Data Formats: JSON, Markdown tables
  • Size: 30+ free zones, ~500 activity mappings
  • Location: PostgreSQL (UAE North)
  • Access Method: Structured query + semantic search
  • Sensitivity: public
  • Update Frequency: Monthly (free zones update policies frequently)
  • Versioning: Timestamped snapshots with diff tracking
Knowledge

Document Checklist Library

Low

Stage-by-stage document requirements for every step of business setup: initial approval, license, visa, banking, and compliance. Includes attestation requirements and Arabic translation rules.

  • Risk: Low
  • Data Formats: JSON, PDF templates
  • Size: ~200 checklists covering 14 setup stages
  • Location: S3 (me-south-1)
  • Access Method: REST API with stage/jurisdiction filters
  • Sensitivity: internal
  • Update Frequency: Bi-monthly
  • Versioning: Semantic versioning per checklist
Knowledge

Fee Schedule & Timelines

Low

Up-to-date government fee schedules, processing timelines, and cost estimator for all business setup stages across mainland and free zones. Includes historical processing time data.

  • Risk: Low
  • Data Formats: JSON, CSV
  • Size: ~1,200 fee line items across 30+ jurisdictions
  • Location: DynamoDB (me-south-1)
  • Access Method: REST API with jurisdiction/stage filters
  • Sensitivity: public
  • Update Frequency: Monthly (fees change with government circulars)
  • Versioning: Append-only with effective dates

Annotations

Dubai Business Setup System
Multi-agent system for end-to-end company formation in Dubai, UAE. The Orchestrator guides the client through 7 major phases: 1. Jurisdiction selection (mainland vs free zone) 2. Document collection and validation 3. Trade name and license registration 4. Office lease and Ejari registration 5. Visa and immigration processing 6. Corporate bank account and tax registration 7. Ongoing compliance (AML, ESR, UBO, tax) Edge styles: Solid = delegation/orchestration Dashed = API calls to government portals Dotted = knowledge/data access Color coding matches agent-to-tool groupings.
